title: melandrium dioicum subsp. divaricatum
intro: Melandrium dioicum subsp. divaricatum, also known as Alpine Catchfly, is a plant species in the Caryophyllaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Alpine Catchfly is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
